message:
feat(python): numpy.ma.maximum_fill_value wrapper parity (franken_numpy-j073)

Add fnp_python.maximum_fill_value(obj) that constructs the opposite-
polarity reduction identity of minimum_fill_value directly in Rust via
a dtype switch: bool→False, signed ints→MIN, unsigned ints→0, floats→
-inf, complex→(-inf, -inf), datetime/timedelta→-i64::MAX (one past
NaT so max reductions never collide with the NaT sentinel). Structured
/ object / Unicode dtypes fall back to numpy.

Mirrors the minimum_fill_value wrapper's contract: accepts an ndarray,
masked array, or bare np.dtype, and returns the numpy-equivalent
sentinel.

Covered by one pyo3 oracle test with eight scenarios comparing repr
against np.ma.maximum_fill_value: float64, int32, uint64, bool,
complex128, masked f64 array, bare np.dtype('float32'), and
datetime64[D].

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
